lines.english: I am worthless - please save me! ||1||Pause||
He has not performed any service to the Creator Lord. ||1||
O God, Your Name is the Purifier of sinners.
O God, You are the Great Giver, the Inner-knower, the Searcher of hearts.
Tastes and pleasures, conflicts and jealousy, and intoxication with Maya
The body of the egotistical human is perishable. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਨ ਸੰਗਿ ਲਾਗਿ ਰਤਨ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: ein sang laag ratan janam gavaaeaa |3|
lines.english: - attached to these, the jewel of human life is wasted. ||3||
Forsaking everything, Nanak has entered His Sanctuary. ||4||13||19||
The Sovereign Lord King is the Destroyer of pain, the Life of the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੇਖਤ ਚਾਖਤ ਕਹੀਅਤ ਅੰਧਾ ਸੁਨੀਅਤ ਸੁਨੀਐ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: pekhat chaakhat kaheeat andhaa suneeat suneeai naahee |
lines.english: He sees with his eyes, but he is called blind; he hears, but he does not hear.
Soohee, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਾਨੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har har naam jap amrit baanee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Chant the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, and the Ambrosial Word of His Bani. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਰਿ ਜਿਤੁ ਛੁਟਹਿ ਪਰਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: so kichh kar jit chhutteh paraanee |
lines.english: Do only those deeds which will save you, O mortal being.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਕਟਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਕਉ ਜਾਣੈ ਦੂਰੇ ਪਾਪੀ ਪਾਪ ਕਮਾਹੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: nikatt vasat kau jaanai doore paapee paap kamaahee |1|
lines.english: And the One who dwells near at hand, he thinks that He is far away; the sinner is committing sins. ||1||
Nothing shall go along with you. ||2||
You are forever imbued with the love of horses and mansions.
but it is so very filthy; it shall receive its punishment from the Messenger of Death. ||3||
You may clean and decorate the vessel of clay,
You are bound by sexual desire, anger, greed and emotional attachment.
I am a stone, sinking down - please, rescue me! ||5||14||20||
Hear this prayer of Nanak, O Lord;
You are sinking down into the great pit. ||4||
Soohee, Fifth Mehl:
One who remains dead while yet alive understands God.
Listen, O friend - this is how to cross over the terrifying world-ocean.
He meets that humble being according to the karma of his past actions. ||1||
There is no other to know, except for the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਧੂ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਉਚਰੀਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mil saadhoo har naam uchareeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meet with the Holy, and chant the Lord's Name||1||Pause||
Know the value of the beginning and the end. ||3||
So realize that the Supreme Lord God is within each and every heart. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਰੈ ਸੋਈ ਭਲ ਮਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: jo kichh karai soee bhal maanai |
lines.english: Whatever He does, accept that as good.
Soohee, Fifth Mehl:
The Guru is the Transcendent Lord, the Creator Lord.
within whose heart the Lord dwells. ||4||15||21||
Says Nanak, I am a sacrifice to that humble being,

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਖੁ ਦਰਦੁ ਇਸੁ ਤਨ ਤੇ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: dookh darad is tan te jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Pain and suffering shall leave this body. ||1||Pause||
He gives His Support to the entire Universe. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਣ ਕਮਲ ਮਨ ਧਿਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur ke charan kamal man dhiaae |
lines.english: Meditate within your mind on the Lotus Feet of the Guru.
He reunites those who were separated for countless incarnations. ||2||
The True Guru saves the drowning being from the terrifying world-ocean.
By great good fortune, one obtains the dust of the feet of the True Guru.
Your mind shall come to have peace, pleasure and poise. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee sevaa karahu din raat |
lines.english: Serve the Guru, day and night.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰ ਕਉ ਸਦ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਵੈ ॥੪॥੧੬॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak gur kau sad bal jaavai |4|16|22|
lines.english: Nanak is forever a sacrifice to the True Guru. ||4||16||22||
Soohee, Fifth Mehl:
I am a sacrifice to my True Guru.
He is the Inner-knower, the Searcher of all hearts. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਠ ਪਹਰ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਈਐ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aatth pehar har har jas gaaeeai |1|
lines.english: Twenty-four hours a day, I sing the Praises of the Lord, Har, Har. ||1||
Meditate in remembrance on God, your Lord and Master.
By the Grace of the Saints, the Lord comes to dwell within the mind,
and live a lifestyle which is true, perfect and spotless. ||2||
So love the Lord's Lotus Feet,
and the sins of countless incarnations are eradicated. ||3||
Please be Merciful, O God, O Merciful to the meek.
Nanak begs for the dust of the Saints. ||4||17||23||
